steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The phrase to the left of the blank—”Having proportionally large populations”—is a gerund phrase that serves as the subject. The right side is a predicate adjective (“common for national capitals”).

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A gerund phrase used as the subject is grammatically singular and therefore requires a singular verb: “is.” The other options use plural forms (“have been,” “are,” “were”), which violate subject-verb agreement. That matches choice B — B is correct.
